sections.paragraph:
ἐκλύω 1 aor. ἐξέλυσα LXX; pf. ptc. ἐκλελυκότα 2 Macc 12:18. Pass.: 1 fut. ἐκλυθήσομαι; 1 aor. ἐξελύθην; pf. ἐκλέλυμαι LXX (Hom. et al.; pap, LXX; TestSol 18:5 P, ἔγκλυσον for ἔγκλεισον; TestJob 30:1; TestZeb 2:4 v.l.; JosAs 10:16 cod. A and Pal. 364 for ἀπεβάλετο). In lit. freq. in the sense ‘loose from someth., set free’, also ‘relax’. In our lit. only in the pass. w. act. sense: be exhausted in strength, become weary, give out (Hippocr., X. et al.; Epict. 2, 19, 20; Phlegon: 257 Fgm. 36, I, 2 Jac.; Vett. Val. 18, 23; 126, 28; LXX; Philo, Virt. 88; Jos., Ant. 5, 134; 13, 233): fr. hunger (Diod S 19, 49, 2; PTebt 798, 7 [II b.c.] 2 Km 16:2; 17:29; La 2:19; 1 Macc 3:17) Mt 15:32; Mk 8:3. Of the waist be ungirded D 16:1. θερίσομεν μὴ ἐκλυόμενοι we will reap if we do not give out Gal 6:9; lose courage (Dt 20:3; 1 Macc 9:8) μηδὲ ἐκλύου do not lose heart Hb 12:5 (Pr 3:11); fully ἐ. ταῖς ψυχαῖς (Polyb. 20, 4, 7; 29, 17, 4; cp. Diod S 20, 1, 4) vs. 3.—M-M. TW. Spicq.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

fut. fut. = fut.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

someth. someth. = something

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

act. act. = active

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Phlegon Phlegon , II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. fr. = from

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 306
